full_text_rationale: Full text describes Group-Evolving Agents maintaining an archive and sharing evolutionary traces, patches, logs, outcomes, and learned artifacts across parent and offspring coding agents, with SWE-bench Verified and Polyglot evaluation.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 010; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Zhaotian Weng, Antonis Antoniades, Deepak Nathani, Zhen Zhang, Xiao Pu, Xin Eric Wang
titulo: Group-Evolving Agents: Open-Ended Self-Improvement via Experience Sharing
formato_armazenamento: Archive of evolving agent groups plus shared group-level experience, including framework code patches, predicted task patches, tool-use logs, workflows, execution outcomes, evaluation results, and learned artifacts.
framework_agente: Group-Evolving Agents (GEA) open-ended self-improvement framework with group-level parent selection and experience sharing
llm_utilizado: Claude Haiku 4.5, Claude Sonnet 4.5; OpenHands+GPT-5 and Aider+GPT-5 used for human-designed framework comparisons
metodo_avaliacao: benchmark; self-evolution comparison; transfer analysis; robustness analysis; cost analysis
benchmarks_utilizados: SWE-bench Verified, SWE-bench Verified-Mini, Polyglot, 10-task sanity checks
metricas_desempenho: pass@1 success, final benchmark score, iterations to fix framework-level bugs, number of evolved agents, ancestor diversity, code changes, cost estimates
metricas_cl: Archive and ancestor diversity; top-agent lineage depth; experience sharing across parent group members; robustness to framework-level bugs
baseline_comparado: DGM-like self-evolving baseline, Darwin Godel Machine lineage, OpenHands+GPT-5, Aider+GPT-5, human-designed framework comparisons
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: GEA runs 30 iterations on SWE-bench and 20 on Polyglot, using groups of size K=2 and KNN M=4. The best GEA agent integrates 17 unique ancestors, about 28.3% of the population.
custo_por_tarefa: Full-run estimates are about USD 13,000 per method on SWE-bench and USD 1,500 on Polyglot; per-60-task evaluation examples include USD 370 for Claude Sonnet 4.5 on SWE-bench and USD 60 on Polyglot.
resultado_principal: GEA reaches 71.0% on SWE-bench Verified and 88.3% on Polyglot, outperforming the DGM-like baseline at 56.7% and 68.3% under the same evolved-agent budget.
desempenho_vs_baseline: GEA nearly matches OpenHands+GPT-5 on SWE-bench Verified (71.0% versus 71.8%) and exceeds Aider+GPT-5 on Polyglot (88.3% versus 52.0%). It fixes framework-level bugs in 1.4 iterations on average versus 5.0 for DGM trials.
custo_vs_baseline: GEA and the DGM-style baseline use the same number of evolved agents, so evaluation cost is reported as similar under the staged benchmark protocol.
limitacoes: Evaluation is expensive and relies on staged benchmark subsets; results depend on model choice, group size, parent-selection settings, and benchmark-specific evolution dynamics.
ameacas_validade: Staged selection from small sanity and mini-benchmark subsets may influence which agents reach full evaluation; benchmark scores depend on tool environments and large model APIs; human-designed framework comparisons are not identical training/evolution protocols.
